JournalPad_LW_Epsilon_BackEntrance_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "05C8A55C42B741FCE12D3EB02A1DA44E", "-- Workpad Local Audio Transcript --
 // User: CORBURN, SAMANTHA //
// Position: Shipping & Receiving Associate - Facility Epsilon //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PATTON: Sam, get this shipment over to Lambda on your next run.
<1>CORBURN: Will do. What's in it?</>
PATTON: Come on Sam, it's not your job to know. You've been around long enough now not to ask what's going on in that place, surely?
<1>CORBURN: Sorry, boss.</>
PATTON: If Doc Sinclair thinks you're snooping around, she'll get your ass thrown down the borehole. You have any idea how cushy it is up here compared to working out of Omicron?
<1>CORBURN: I know. Sorry.</>
PATTON: Alright, I'm just trying to look out for you. Use the usual code for this one, OK?
<1>CORBURN: Right. 7924, got it.</>
PATTON: Right, but remember, don't write down the code!
<1>CORBURN: Ok, boss.</>
PATTON: Go on, back to work, Sam.

[ AUDIO ENDS ]")

JournalPad_LW_Epsilon_EmptyRoom.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "9245DA2141064CF8521252B5684DD7B0", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: ANON955
// ANONYMOUS MESSAGING APP
------------------------------------------------------------------------

<2>ANON955: I swear I saw that cleaner break down and cry when she discovered that latest batch of vibrascrubbers missing! ^_^ </>
<1>HEARTBREAKHOTEL: Haha I would have loved to see that!</>
<2>ANON955: We'll get her sacked before too long, that's what she gets for knocking my limited-edition CyberGirl Edition 12 figurine off my desk...</>
<1>HEARTBREAKHOTEL: She'll be more careful in future! Don't mess with us, chica!</>

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_LW_Epsilon_Exterior_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "187858A545C0E0FBC230DEB2EA1D462E", "// FACILITY EPSILON //
// LOCAL AREA ANNOUNCEMENTS //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ ANNOUNCEMENT BEGINS ]

<1>Alright crew, I get that the conveyor system in the guts of this place is pretty cool and all, but you've got to stop putting random junk into the system.</>

<1>Yes, sometimes you can chuck one of those weird little local creatures into the container on this end and it'll come out at the climber for your friend to chase around the place, but SOMETIMES THEY GET CAUGHT IN THE MACHINERY.</>

<1>Next person I catch putting anything into the system that doesn't belong is on cleaning duty for a month, and trust me, that's not a work detail you want.</>

<1>- Patton</>

[ ANNOUNCEMENT ENDS ]")

JournalPad_LW_Epsilon_Exterior_2.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "D7C5484749F9B0A24B1758BBC45741DB", "-- Workpad Audio Messenger --
// User: VIHAAN, CALEB //
// Position: Archaeology Fellow - Facility Lambda //
// TRANSCRIBED RECORDING FOLLOWS //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

LI: Mr. Vihaan, I trust you're well?
<1>VIHAAN: Dr. Li! Yes, very well, thank you! Uh... How are you?</>
LI: Fine, thank you. I'm getting in touch to enquire about your work. You know that Leonard is very passionate about your... bones, and whatnot. He's keen to know how you're getting on.
<1>VIHAAN: Yes! Yes, very well. Things are going well, I mean.</>
LI: Good, I'm glad. Do you need anything up there at Lambda?
<1>VIHAAN: No, no, we have an ample supply of fossil samples thanks to the 'local fauna', heh. Oh, I had heard someone talk about the power system being a bit unreliable, but I believe Chief Engineer Chapman is handling it.</>
LI: Excellent. I'll let Leonard know that things are progressing well. I'm sure he'll be keen to see some tangible pieces for his collection soon.
<1>VIHAAN: No problem, we have some small initial fossils in the final stages of extraction now - the new prospecting bench technology has made the process far easier, thank you for that.</>
LI: Not a problem, keep up the good work. Speak soon, Mr. Vihaan.
<1>VIHAAN: Goodbye, Dr. Li.</>

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_LW_Epsilon_Harlow_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "A0F73CB54530E089090875838D511FE6", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: HARLOW, GRANT //
// Position: Chief Scientist - Facility Kappa //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

HARLOW: Tanya, you know that I'm not one to be overly cautious, but I must once again urge taking particular care today.
<1>LI: Grant, you assured me in the lead-up to today's test that everything was, and I quote: 'absolutely ready'.</>
HARLOW: Well, I, yes... As ready as we can be, given the circumstances. As we discussed, without the perfected construct I cannot guarantee success.
<1>LI: We have no choice, the Board are breathing down my neck; if we fail, we try again.</>
HARLOW: It's just... well... you remember what happened with the very first sample... The blackouts?
<1>LI: You don't need to remind me, Grant. I was physically in the chamber. Unlike you, if I recall.</>
HARLOW: Uh, yes, well. I had to monitor things from the gallery, naturally.
<1>LI: I have to prepare, we'll debrief after the test.</>

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_LW_Epsilon_Loadmaster_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "8869F7FA4DA9036D0B2D1081F9FFC2BF", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: PATTON, ROGER //
// Position: Loadmaster - Facility Epsilon //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PATTON: Jack, it's Roger - embarrassing question, but you've not seen my secure storage key anywhere have you?
<1>XING: Hey Roger. Uh... I don't think so? Remind me what it looks like?</>
PATTON: Small, bronze. Stupidly small, now I think about it.
<1>XING: No, can't say I've seen a key like that lately, sorry. Can you retrace your steps to when you last had it?</>
PATTON: I've been thinking about it, but the only time I left Epsilon recently was to check out that stupid fishing hab that Sorrell set up. 
<1>XING: Maybe shoot him a message?</>
PATTON: Good idea. We've got to bunker down soon, maybe I'll catch him when he comes up from Kappa. Thanks Sam, take it easy.
<1>XING: You too Roger, hope you find the key soon!</>

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_LW_FishingHab_FishingChargePl.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "ED107B8C48C2362D2B1A0288DABA3EA8", "<1>On the workstation you discover plans for a strange device that appears to utilize jury-rigged ARC anti-gravity technology in an unorthodox way...</>\r\n\r\n<1>You think you should be able to make this device at the Electronics Workbench if you can get your hands on the required materials.</>")

JournalPad_LW_FishingHab_Loadmaster.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "1C1ECC9549928C3810850798DCC4601A", "<1>Cindy, I found this key in the grass near the platform, I assume someone dropped it while they were over here. I\'ve put it into the storage chest, I\'ll leave it to you to handle!</>\r\n\r\n<1>- Robert</>")

JournalPad_LW_Kappa_Engineering_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "AECF0E9A4FD942685A5EAEBD3FC5A5E9", "-- Facility Kappa Workstation --
// User: HARLOW, GRANT //
// Position: Chief Scientist - Facility Kappa //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

HARLOW: Dieter. Stanley reported yet another leak here at Kappa, which meant draining the facility, again.
<1>YAR: Dr. Harlow, we're doing the best we can. The entire facility is submerged under thousands of pounds per square foot of water pressure when active.</>
HARLOW: The best you can? I hardly think so. Does Omega spring leaks every couple of weeks? No? Could that be because if they did, everyone would DIE?
<1>YAR: Well, the tolerances for Omega are significantly tighter than for Kappa, naturally.</>
HARLOW: Right. So - the best you can?
<1>YAR: Point taken, Dr. Harlow. I appreciate your frustration, but we're limited in what we can do. The strongest materials are diverted to the deepest facilities, through necessity. It's above my pay grade I'm afraid.</>
HARLOW: Hmph, I'll be raising this with Mr. Lacey in that case - Kappa's work is the cornerstone of the project - a catastrophic flood here has the capability to cripple us.
<1>YAR: Understood, Doctor. I'll come down and you can walk me through Stanley's data.</>
HARLOW: Hm, good. I'll be in touch.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_LW_Kappa_Engineering_2.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "190653754398426CA5EB16B3A815DBA2", "-- Facility Kappa Workstation --
// User: SORRELL, ANDY //
// Position: Research Assistant - Facility Kappa //
// SYSTEM CORRUPTION DETECTED //
// RECOVERED EXCHANGE FOLLOWS //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SORRELL: Sienna, I've done it! Finally!
<1>POLLARD: Wait, you've cracked the extraction issue?</>
SORRELL: What? No, of course not, that's your job!
<1>POLLARD: Then what have you done?</>
SORRELL: I've thought of a name!
<1>POLLARD: ......</>
<1>POLLARD: For the project management AI construct?</>
SORRELL: Yes!
<1>POLLARD: Go on then?</>
SORRELL: 'Self-Taught Autonomous Network-Linked Executive System' - STANLEY, for short.
<1>POLLARD: Andy... that doesn't spell Stanley. 'System' doesn't begin with a 'Y'.</>
SORRELL: Give me a break here! Do you have any idea how hard it is to come up with a fitting acronym?...
<1>POLLARD: Hm, fair enough. The science isn't the hard part, it's the naming the stuff you create that's difficult.</>
SORRELL: Exactly! It's close enough. We're never going to use the full name anyway. I think 'Stanley' has a nice ring to it. Makes him seem... approachable.
<1>POLLARD: Not 'Him', Andy. 'It'.</>
SORRELL: Yes, yes, of course... It.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_LW_Kappa_Exterior_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "F683A11C44A85A487D37619B4317C6A1", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: SORRELL, ANDY //
// Position: Research Assistant - Facility Kappa //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SORRELL: Alright mate! Do you fancy some fishing next rest period?
<1>PARKER: I don't know, I've been picking up extra shifts...</>
SORRELL: Ah come on, you need a break, got to get out of that big metal box you live in! We finished setting up the fishing spot, so it's a lot comfier than before!
<1>PARKER: Hmm, well, it would be nice to be by the water again.</>
SORRELL: Yes! Come on, the jungle is really nice right now, I don't know how the seasons work down here but it's a lot cooler than usual!
<1>PARKER: Ok, ok, fine! I'll meet you at Kappa and you can show me the way.</>
SORRELL: Awesome. It's not far, just across the dam, but we'll go together. Gotta go, Harlow is on the warpath again about this project, speak soon!

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_LW_Lambda_Interior_1.JournalEntryHeader_27_A9E4541846447E9F29: NSLOCTEXT("", "132FA96F47E2CAF8D5660888F6B7641B", "Lambda All Hands: New Staff Member")
JournalPad_LW_Lambda_Interior_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "FC1087BD402D6DBC75BC9EB778B993AB", "// FACILITY LAMBDA //
// LOCAL AREA ANNOUNCEMENTS //
---------------------------------------------------------

[ ANNOUNCEMENT BEGINS ]

<1>Good day everyone,</>

<1>Please be aware that we will soon be receiving a new staff member.</>

<1>Mr Caleb Vihaan joins us an an independent archaeology fellow, fresh from a posting on Procima II, where he was managing a dig-site excavating the infamous Royal Traugr!</>

<1>He has been personally requested for assignment here at Lambda by the higher-ups, so please afford him every courtesy and make sure he has whatever he needs to conduct his work.</>

<1>He has had this message for you all sent ahead of his arrival:</>

<3>“Hello! I’m Caleb, and I’ll be joining you on this expedition shortly! I’m incredibly excited by the chance to work with such talented people, on such a fascinating project (not that I know what everyone is doing - very secretive!) and am very much looking forward to meeting you all and excavating some cool stuff!”</>

<1>I trust that you’ll all make him feel very welcome.</>

<1>Saoirse Sanders</>
<1>Lambda Site Manager</>")

JournalPad_MR_FishingHab_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "DBED2F084EC1256AD0E53D9791F66CDC", "-- Magma Rifts Workstation --
// User: MUNSHI, PHILIP //
// Position: Manufacturing Engineer - Facility Omicron //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TIPPLE: Hey Phil! I got the go-ahead to study some of the local magmafauna, but I can't do it here in Omi. Do you think I could get a little space in your hab?
<1>MUNSHI: I'll need to run it by the others, but if you don't need a lot of space then it shouldn't be a problem. What are you planning?</>
TIPPLE: Standard small aquaculture containment, adapted for the local critters. If you can make it happen for me, I promise to raise a few of those weird creatures you've been using as bait?
<1>MUNSHI: Think I can make it happen! Will get back to you, Col!</>
TIPPLE: Thanks, speak soon!

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_MR_Rho_Engineering_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "F8F60A8D412C2C59BA61E58966B8B25A", "-- Workpad Journal v1.3 --
// User: HOFFMAN, FRANK //
// Position: Senior Engineer - Facility Delta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>This is the third time I've had to reset the ID for one of the teleporters in this stupid, boiling hot facility...</>

<1>These eggheads obviously don't realise what could happen in an emergency situation if they keep switching these IDs around like this... Management can't control the system if the IDs aren't matching up!</>

<1>Someone's going to step into one of these things in Tau and come out into zero atmosphere, underwater, or worse!</>")

JournalPad_OC_CliffbaseHabitat_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "82A4A0E34CEB3A7BF90CC99BB14B65DC", "-- Workpad Messenger --
 // User: SPARKS, CARLOS //
// Position: Mining Supervisor - Facility Beta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SHIN: Sparky, you there?
<1>SPARKS: Reading you, Tony.</>
SHIN: Time to pack up and head back down to Epsilon, can you let your crew know?
<1>SPARKS: Roger that. This the big test they've been talking about?</>
SHIN: Yup. Time to put that training into action, fella.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_OC_CraterFacility.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "EA850362443F49F50F8A3D8DF8E62FA7", "-- Workpad Messenger --
 // User: SHIN, ANTHONY //
// Position: Junior Security Officer - Facility Delta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SHIN: Epsilon, this is Delta.
<1>PARKER: Reading you, Delta.</>
SHIN: The rest of the crew are on their way down to you as scheduled, but we've got one person still out in the field.
<1>PARKER: Who is it?</>
SHIN: Jackson. He's pinging at the transmitter tower just behind the base, but won't answer comms.
<1>PARKER: Goddamnit.</>
SHIN: I'm heading out to find him, just hold off the test for a little longer until we can get back down to you.
<1>PARKER: You think I have the power to make that happen? Hurry.</>
SHIN: Going now.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_OC_CraterFacility_Corpses.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "CA8D69784EDFDE9BB1A32BB39D3C1A97", "-- Workpad Local Audio Transcript --
 // User: JACKSON, THOMAS //
// Position: Mining Engineer - Facility Delta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

JACKSON: Oh thank God, someone found me! Who is that?
<1>SHIN: Jackson! What the hell are you still doing out here? We have to get down to Epsilon!</>
JACKSON: Tony! My suit has gone haywire; life support and comms aren't working! I couldn't leave the oxygen generator, I thought you'd all left me out here!
<1>SHIN: Not a chance. </>
JACKSON: Tony, your nose...
<1>SHIN: What? Oh sh-</>

[ AUDIO ENDS ]")

JournalPad_OC_CraterFacility_Exterior.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "8DB1B8F24B81FCE65B9430922BE3742D", "-- Workpad Messenger --
 // User: BROUSSARD, GEORGE //
// Position: Shipping & Receiving Supervisor - Facility Delta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

HARLOW: Where's that latest shipment? Our continued development relies on the shipments we require arriving when they're supposed to, not days late.
<1>BROUSSARD: Sorry, Dr Harlow, I will check in on that for you.</>
<1>BROUSSARD: Your shipment of gold is on the way as I write, I apologise for the delay, there were... complications at Drill Site V.</>
HARLOW: Do you think I care about that? Do you think these vital electronics are going to fabricate themselves, without that gold?
<1>BROUSSARD: It's coming down to the crater and we'll send it straight down to you, apologies Dr Harlow.</>
HARLOW: Just get it here now.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

JournalPad_OC_Vihaan_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:

Kryptos_Atrium_Log.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "B30B0DE8460A8D0DD7C2488D8F4A41C2", "Transcript of a recording found within Medical Control, in Site Kryptos.

-- RECORDING BEGINS --

<1>Hello, Mr. Vihaan. I’m Dr. Harlow - Grant. Can I call you Caleb?</>

<3>Uh. Sure. Is this the Kryptos project? Dr. Li said my skillset was required here… Have you found some new fossils?</>

<1>That’s right, you’re uniquely suited to help us here, Caleb! We really appreciate you making the trip.</>

<2>Would you come this way, sir? Please follow me and we’ll get you situated.</>

<3>Oh, okay. No problem, lead the way.</>

-- RECORDING ENDS --")

Kryptos_MedicalChief_Terminal.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "6D10C514415264626C29D5B874BF5272", "An unsent message, found in the Drafts directory of the workstation within the Kryptos Medical Chief's Office.

<1>Get STANLEY to generate outline for facility-wide email announcing leadership changes. Authoritative and strong. Make it seem like I wrote it.</>

<1>Points to cover:</>

<1>1: Everyone needs to work harder - we have to make up for that useless oaf leaving and screwing everything up.</>

<1>2: Reinforce my new leadership and superiority - he was always undermining me, now I'm the one in charge and we'll do things my way.</>

<1>3: Finish strong with a motivational quote or similar, inspire confidence and show them all that I'm just as good as he was (better!).</>

<2>GRANT, REMEMBER TO CHANGE TITLE BEFORE SENDING OUT! CANNOT MAKE A MISTAKE NOW.</>")

Kryptos_ScanningSuite_Log_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "C2D0E4A9463186BFC97F209FC3554A8F", "Transcript of a recording found within the Scanning Suite of Site Kryptos.

-- RECORDING BEGINS --

<1>Dr. Gerard Shaw, commencing scan and upload procedure on candidate number six.</>

<2>Subject brain thaw process is complete, doctor.</>

<1>Thank you. Boot the latest version of the intelligence construct ready for cognitive merge, please.</>

<2>Yes, doctor. Booting version 1.4 now.</>

<1>Good. Administering wakening serum.</>
<1>Hello there. What’s your name?</>

<3>Caleb Vihaan… Where am I?</>

<1>It’s OK, Caleb. You’re safe.</>

<3>Wait... Dr. Shaw? Is that you? Why can’t I see?</>

<1>It’s just very dark, don’t worry.</>

<3>No, no… Why can’t I move my arms? My... my legs?</>

<1>Calm down, Caleb. Deep breaths.</>

<3>I… I can’t… breathe.</>

<1>Nurse, commence initial consciousness scan.</>

<3>What are you doing to me? WHERE AM I?</>

<1>Ten CCs carlamine, nurse. Aligning scanning nodes on subject.</>

<3>WHAT’S HAPPENING? WHAT ARE YOU DOING TO ME?!</>

<1>Mute cerebral cortex translation functions, please.</>
<1>Thank you. It was getting a bit hard to think.</>

<2>Doctor… is it still… ‘awake’?</>

<1>Yes, yes. We just can’t hear any more. Full concentration, please - this is going to be a long procedure.</>

-- RECORDING ENDS --")

Kryptos_SurgerySuite_Log_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "ED6CBEF143C280AB7C3AD9B43360E534", "Transcript of a recording found within the Surgery Suite of Site Kryptos.

-- RECORDING BEGINS --

<1>"Mr Vihaan, Kryptos is located in a rich area of fossilized creatures, but the risk of biological contamination is high - we’re going to need to give you a small set of immunizations before you begin your work here."</>

<3>"Sounds reasonable… This all looks a bit overkill for some injections, doc?"</>

<1>"Ah, the equipment is all just ready for any kind of medical emergency we were to face here - nothing to worry about."</>

<3>"Right. Well equipped here, aren’t you!"</>

<1>"Oh, yes. Mr. Virtus is putting all possible resources towards making this particular project a success."</>

<3>"Well, I’m glad to help however I can."</>

<1>"Good man, Caleb. Now, lie back and just relax. You’ll just feel a small scratch."</>
<1>"There, not so bad, eh?"</>
<1>"Caleb? Can you hear me?"</>

<1>"Nurse, time to scrub up, please."</>

-- RECORDING ENDS --")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Epsilon_ClimberStatus.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "07CDAAB74873062C4A158CAC3E88FD4F", "-- Facility Epsilon Workstation --

// SCARPA BOREHOLE SCHEMATIC OVERLAY //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>Complex looking blueprints run across the screen, showing the Scarpa Borehole and Climber in intricate detail.</>

<1>You can see technical data and materials required for each facility within the Borehole - the manpower and resources required to construct this vast network of facilities was enormous.</>

<1>At the lowest, deepest point on the plans are facilities labeled as follows:</>

<1>- BOREHOLE FACILITY: Omicron</>
<1>- POWER FACILITY: Rho</>
<1>- COMMUNICATIONS FACILITY: Sigma</>
<1>- MATERIAL REFINEMENT FACILITY: Tau</>
<1>- MATERIALS RESEARCH FACILITY: Iota</>
<1>- SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH FACILITY: Omega</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Epsilon_Ncuti_Intro.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "680F00B541DEE2880B8782A7DA383101", "<3>// Confidential biographical source material. For personal memoir use only! //</>

<1>62 hours of travel. 3 transit jumps. Last of which they had the temerity to conduct with me inside a container pod, smuggled like petty contraband! 15 meals of ration #6, despite my stated preference of NO CURRY, and an overly chatty travel partner...</>
<1>This had better be worth it.</>

<1>Preliminary reports on this so-called "mycelial anomaly" (note, adjust designation to something more suitable for wide-spread academic use) are fascinating.</>
<1>Dr. Shujin was always too excitable and lacked proper scientific detachment, but I'll give credit where due for the wisdom to call in those with deeper insights and more expertise.</>

<1>This is a remarkable operation. Even I hadn't heard a whisper about it. Remember to ask about the initial team selection - why was I not requested on inception?</>

<1>Note to self, encrypt these notes after inspecting Theta.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Epsilon_Ncuti_Room.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "95F10FB6400957E0E8DBB08C809F5FF4", "New diary entry with more lore/character fleshing out")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Epsilon_Parker_Quarters.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "EB2473FA4EB19945E10D70B05D0B3800", "-- Workstation Journal v1.3 --
// User: ANON955 //
// Position: [UNKNOWN] //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>Today's rant; I've got to stop working with imbeciles. I go to all this trouble to set up secure firewalled terminals and workpads, and they go and leave them lying around, or even forget to use their anonymous credentials?!</>

<1>Do they even realise the risks we're taking doing this stuff? If we were found out... Ugh. Maybe I need to quit while I'm ahead. The credits are really good though... People love booze.</>

<1>Oh, and the higher-ups denied my bloody safe-working request! Giving me who-knows-what herbicide stuff to deploy without safety gear? They didn't even say what it was for! I'm getting sick of all this.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Epsilon_Tear_Paper.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "91B952EB43DF94C4A25B0399B0A60B3B", "<1>This communication is designated MANAGEMENT EYES ONLY. </>

<1>Awareness, distribution, consumption, or acknowledgement of this communication by unauthorized personal is punishable by the penalties defined in sub-section 21A of the Compulsary Employee Agreements document: "Permanant Revocation of Corporeal Actuality".</>

Team, the existence of protocols for dealing with the winding down of projects such as this is completely normal and expected. Especially for projects operating in the kind of adversarial circumstances we do.

We've all built relationships that would make the final steps of the TEAR_PAPER protocol difficult. I suggest you use this fact to motivate your teams to re-double their efforts so we can demonstrate the viability of our approach in the next extraction test.

I trust this additional context puts the topic to rest.

End of message.
Tanya Li, Expedition Lead.")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Lambda_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "B97587F24A3934F2C5DACA86C320C285", "-- Facility Lambda Workstation --
// User: CHAPMAN, LISA //
// Position: Chief Power Engineer //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CHAPMAN: Tanya, to what do I owe the honour?
<1>LI: Hello Lisa. I was informed that there might be some sort of issue with the power system at Lambda? I wanted to check in personally.</>
CHAPMAN: No problem, not to worry. It's all in hand. I'm dealing with it myself.
<1>LI: Good, but indulge me - what is the issue?</>
CHAPMAN: Honestly, it's nothing to worry about. The standalone Quarinium reactor we installed was just having some teething problems.
<1>LI: Go on?</>
CHAPMAN: Well, the fuel rods we're getting from Tau are just a little off-spec for the casing sleeves we have. It's not a problem, it would take years to have any noticeable effect.
<1>LI: So there are no possible safety issues?</>
CHAPMAN: No, no. We even installed an ARMS system - that's an Automated Reactor Maintenance System - in case any maintenance is required. You could replace almost the entire unit without even going into the same room.
CHAPMAN: That said, the need for a standalone reactor here at Lambda was unforeseen, as I've previously mentioned.
<1>LI: We've already discussed it, Lisa, your objection was noted. There was no alternative with the corrosive nature of the soil in the vicinity. I'll raise the Tau issue with Mr. Lacey at the next engineering roundtable.</>
CHAPMAN: Understood Dr. Li.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

LW_ExpeditionLog_MR_Rho_Exterior_PowerRe.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "3C80E82A4FB2F7227E8EBDA287644283", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: TAYLOR, SANDRA //
// Position: Senior Engineer - Facility Rho //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TAYLOR: Lisa, I've checked out the dodgy relay and confirmed the fault. Shouldn't take long.
<1>CHAPMAN: Good, well done Sandra. Anything else?</>
TAYLOR: Well, I wanted to flag that the instability in the lava spillways seems to be increasing. I have doubts about the long-term stability of the infrastructure here.
<1>CHAPMAN: Likewise, but hopefully we won't be here long enough for it to matter.</>
TAYLOR: Hm. Ok. Just... With a major tectonic shift, any of these relays could go down. That's a loss of power to all the rest of the facilities...
<1>CHAPMAN: Lucky we have you to send out and fix things then, eh? Look, I agree, but it's been signed off by Tanya herself. No extra redundancy. It's too expensive. Come back in and get out of the heat, the lockdown is starting soon.</>
TAYLOR: Roger that. I've left a buildkit here in case we need a total refit on the relay later - saves me lugging it back down. Heading back now.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Mu_AntigenDrone.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "5BB72EA94814A6CE5F2809A094C8D471", "<1>Hey Tanz, I know you told me to stop sending you samples. And ok, sure, 11 of the same plant was a lot (they all had different shaped spots though!), but Parker said you were looking for plants that glow and are blue. I found some!</>

<1>They smell good. Like, REALLY good.</>

<1>I've asked Yar for a delivery drone assignment so I can send you the sample (just one, promise!), but he's being all pissy about "essential supplies" and "maintaining mission critical power levels".</>

<1>And just in case I can swing the drone, ignore the little slice missing. I snipped a bit out because I think Elsabeth likes the smell too. She perked right up when I gave her some.</>

<1>I've attached scanner details so you can ID it in the field, along with the coordinates for where I found them.</>

<1>Talk soon, ok!</>
<1>Gustav</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Mu_HerbicideLog.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "5982C6F448482A6EF19AB196FA772570", "<1>[Security Officer] {Parker}, your submission of a 'Personal Health / Wellbeing Risk Mitigation Adjustment' (form #73-T) via our Administrative Support Request portal has been received and reviewed.</>

<1>Your directive remains as previously issued; you are to detonate the herbicidal dispersment device INSIDE the Mu facility tomorrow, and BEFORE the AM team shift begins.</>
<1>I can assure you that no damage will be caused to any critical power generation equipment or project assets.</>

<1>Additionally, sufficient study of side-effects was conducted by the team responsible for manufacturing of the formula in the biolab at Theta - with acceptable levels of health / wellbeing realignment detected.</>
<1>Management is confident that operations will not be meaningfully impacted.</>

<1>Further, your request to alter the detonation timeline to accomodate your 'morale building fishing exercise' is denied.</>

<1>Administrative Support Request #9,629 is now closed and automatically marked as 'Gratefully resolved by submitting party'.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Mu_MobileLab.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "B2FE2D3341C9BEBAF08F43A83BB9B1C6", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: SANDFORD, RUPERT //
// Position: Chief Custodian //
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

KINGSTON: Rupe, it's bad enough we have to clean up after these slobs, but now my cleaning supplies are going missing.
<1>SANDFORD: Missing? Holly, are you sure you've not just misplaced them?</>
KINGSTON: Twelve vibrascrubbers in six weeks? I'm good at my job, Rupert. Wear and tear on supplies, obviously, but I'm not losing stuff all the time.
<1>SANDFORD: So what are you suggesting?</>
KINGSTON: Someone is messing with me.
<1>SANDFORD: That's a bold accusation, Holly... These people are professionals, why would they do that?</>
KINGSTON: Don't ask me why these neurotic freaks do anything! They're a law unto themselves! The stuff they're doing here...
<1>SANDFORD: Alright, that's enough. Keep your stuff in the storage room in Epsilon for now, and we'll see if that stops things disappearing. Code to the door is 6980.</>
KINGSTON: Thanks, boss.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Mu_PowerStats.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "D773FFCC43D5E924D3E7DB978A35A3F3", "-- Facility Mu Workstation --

// LOCAL POWER STATISTICS //
---------------------------------------

<1>Schematics and diagrams flow across the screen, showing a map of local facilities and their connections to Mu's power grid.</>

<1>LOCAL POWER GENERATION: 65% CAPACITY</>

<1>- EPSILON: Connected.</>
<2>- SCARPA CLIMBER: !Warning! No powered connection to Omicron!</>
<1>- THETA: Connected.</>
<1>- LAMBDA: No connection. Local power generation in effect.</>
<2>- KAPPA: Connected. !Warning! Watercooling systems will fail at 40% capacity!</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Mu_SolarPanels.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "464EA73B4633394C7E56FF814F82604E", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: YAR, DIETER //
// Position: Senior Engineer - Facility Mu //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>Rupert, I appreciate that your guys have a pretty crappy job cleaning all the junk off the equipment across these facilities, but one of our panels at Mu was damaged again in the last couple of days.</>

<1>I'm sure I don't have to explain again how much these things cost - it's not exactly simple to modify standard photovoltaic panels to operate on a totally different wavelength from sunlight to generate power from bioluminescent flora...</>

<1>If your team aren't sure of the best way to clean these things, we can find time to conduct a training session. They just... really need to stop breaking them!</>

<1>Get back to me when you can, please.</>
<1>Dieter.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Mu_Tear_Paper.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "FB0F35F641A46C13CD16A2B0CC59084A", "-- Facility Mu Command Workstation --
// User: YAR, DIETER //
// Position: Senior Engineer - Facility Mu //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>What the hell, Lisa?!</>

<1>I know the last time you got your hands dirty was back when you were mistaking E = (1/2)CV² for i = C * dv/dt - but I shouldn't have to tell you that adding a remotely activated command to 'lock down' a capacitor storing upwards of 3.2 terrawatts is a terrible idea.</>

<1>And yes, I know you want it in place before the test in a few hours! But I don't even know who's in this Tear_Paper security group you want it assigned to.</>

<1>Come down and tell me what the hell is going on.</>

<1>Dieter.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Tau_Stuck.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "A0B18AF34EBF4870FC4369BD1B785E71", "-- Workpad Journal v1.3 --
// User: CLANCY, CALLUM //
// Position: Manufacturing Engineer - Facility Tau //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>I can't believe it. What I said was going to happen all along has actually happened, and now I'm stuck here.</>

<1>I've been telling them, again and again - "These doors are faulty, they break down too often and if they all fail, we're going to have people stuck."</>
<1>Well, guess what's happened. To me, no less. It's almost poetic.</>

<1>"Callum, don't worry, we know the doors aren't great but they won't all fail at once!"</>
<1>"Callum, even if they did, we would get whoever's stuck inside out quickly!"</>

<1>Well, what about when everyone's gone off to Omicron because 'management' decided not to build a bunker here in Tau? EVEN RHO GOT A BUNKER. GODDAMNIT.</>
<1>It's so classic. I thought this expedition might be a fresh start, but it's ended up just like everything else. It's always the little guy who gets the raw end of the deal. The scientists are at the top of the pyramid, and here we are, the manual workers, machinists and engineers, at the bottom. As always.</>

<1>If you find this after you come out of your cosy little bunker after leaving me trapped in here, and my skin has melted and I'm half-braindead, know that my ghost is haunting you and you'll never know peace. OOOOOOOO</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Theta_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "398E069547A2B2C74D27598C3A45CF10", "-- Workpad Journal v1.3 --
 // User: SHUJIN, TAI //
// Position: Senior Botanist - Facility Theta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>We have now brought samples of the mycelial fungus in-house from the mobile lab so that we have our full suite of testing equipment available for use.</>

<1>Dr. Sinclair has put in a request for additional air filtering and extraction equipment due to the sheer quantity of spores that the fungus emits, which I believe has been granted - the request was prompted by the accidental exposure of other kinds of local plant matter (part of our agricultural and hydroponic farming research cadre) to the fungal spores...</>

<1>Fairly rapidly, the exposed specimens appeared to decay, discolouring. Early signs of fungal growth were present on the affected organisms, suggesting that there may be a way for this fungus to infect other types of life within its native biome.</>

<1>It does appear that this 'pseudofungus' becomes... agitated (can I even use that word to describe this organism? It surely isn't 'thinking'...) when removed from its native soil and subjected to tests in our laboratory. My hypothesis is that this is what gives rise to the significant quantity of spores released.</>

<1>Note to self: We may need to hot-swap samples of this organism in and out of the testing labs as they become more 'agitated', in order to avoid a potentially dangerous build-up of spores. Within the lab I can't imagine there's much risk, but a sufficient quantity of spores at a high enough density could have an adverse effect on the local ecosystem.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Theta_2.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "6ADA4CDF4B08EAE801BC44B9DEBD6E86", "-- Research Journal - Facility Theta --
 // User: SHUJIN, TAI //
// Position: Senior Botanist - Facility Theta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>Recent experimentation has yielded an incredible discovery.</>

<1>It appears that our little 'pseudofungus' friends contain some sort of enzyme that boosts the development and growth of plants to which it is exposed, by an order of magnitude... I suppose it provides an explanation for such a cornucopia of plant life this deep within the planet's crust.</>

<1>The implications of this finding are potentially enormous - both to the expedition and, dare I say it, humanity itself. We could significantly increase the output of our hydroponic farms here in Theta, but bigger than that... The end of hunger, with crop yields and growth iterations larger and faster than humanity has ever seen.</>

<1>We don't want to get ahead of ourselves, but initial tests are proving promising. Dr. Sinclair has asked Dr. Li herself to attend the facility next week in order to present our findings. More work is required, but this could be our ticket to a Weissman prize in Biology... Just think of it!</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Theta_3.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "2DF4DD01440225193AA38EA64F460579", "-- Workpad Journal v1.3 --
 // User: TANZ, KRISTOF //
// Position: Junior Botanist - Facility Theta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>Sometimes I like to just sit by the tree within the biodome and think for a bit.</>

<1>I don't know why, but often I'll suddenly discover the solution to a problem I'm working on, or have an idea of a new experiment to conduct...</>

<1>As far as I'm aware, the expedition calls them 'Greatwood' trees, and I can certainly see why if it's due to their stature.</>

<1>There are several other huge specimens within the local area, though many of them still maintain the natural pools around their bases. Our own resident had its pool filled in as part of the facility construction...</>

<1>Speaking of the pools, I stuck my hand into one the last time I was over by Epsilon and had the strangest feeling... A sense of bliss washed over me.</>

<1>I could have sworn I had a large cut on my index finger as well, but maybe it had already healed, because it wasn't there when it came into my mind again after that...</>

<1>Anyway, I'm rambling... I guess I just really like these trees.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Theta_4.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "3CCF762D4CE5A3E8371ADBA88BB81950", "-- Workpad Journal v1.3 --
 // User: SHUJIN, TAI //
// Position: Senior Botanist - Facility Theta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>I've been conducting a little side project in my spare time - call it... an insurance policy.</>

<1>Concern has been growing in my mind about the 'pseudofungus' spores and their speed of their release and effect on the human body; one of the lab technicians failed to correctly follow containment procedure the other day, releasing a dense cloud of spores, which they promptly inhaled(!)...</>

<1>There seem to be no long-lasting effects (that we have yet seen, in any case) and recovery was swift, but said technician was immediately incapacitated and almost incapable of any functioning for a good five to ten minutes.</>

<1>My concern is that, were a larger-scale breach to occur, the entire facility staff could be incapacitated and unable to escape. God knows what the effects of longer-term exposure would look like...</>

<1>Anyway, I've been looking at creating something of an 'antigen' against the effect of these spores which we could administer to the staff to avoid this situation occurring, should the worst happen.</>

<1>It's yet to be trialled, but I think that the natural resistance of local flora is the key to protection from the spores - they have proven to be remarkably resistant.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Theta_5.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "613BD3A44E8DDF79FB2BBFB2FB3A3D57", "<1>Subject ID: Growth Sample 71p. Subject status: Clear.</>
<1>Material test status: Positive.</>

<1>After application of herbicidal formula 18a, complete regression of invading bioluminescent flora was observed. Moreover, material structural analysis revealed no traces of growth promotors.</>

<1>Additional testing of the formula on bunny 903g5 is ongoing, however tests on bunnies 817p2 and 387m1 indicate acceptable levels of genetic abnormalities and metabolic permutations.</>

<1>Use of the herbicide for facility clearing operations is Approved.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Theta_6.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "293728834A26AC3D6A3003ADF7A4B9C5", "-- Facility Theta Workstation --
// WELFARE HABITAT - COMMUNAL TERMINAL //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- FACILITY-WIDE ANNOUNCEMENT -

<1>Good day, team.</>

<1>I wanted to take an opportunity, on the day of our colleagues' latest foray beyond the bounds of scientific progress, to remind us all of our most important mission here at Theta.</>

<1>I am sure that you will share my opinion that our beloved calling - that being the Biological Sciences - while less 'flashy' than the specialities of our colleagues in Omega, is nonetheless a vital discipline for this expedition and that we all fulfil a critical role in the 'ecosystem' of these facilities.</>

<1>While we here at Theta are also on the brink of our own historic breakthroughs, we must nonetheless continue to perform our key role - the production of edible food products.</>

<1>The work we do here enables our colleagues and friends in our sister facilities to do their own, so I ask you to keep this in mind moving forward and resist unsanctioned non-critical work that does not further our core mission here.</>

<1>Together, for the future.</>
<1> - Dr. Sinclair</>

[ ENDS ]")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Theta_Corpse.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "3190E7A94D6EA85E5BC9FC9D72861D61", "<1>Subject ID: Bunny 39f6. Subject status: expired.</>
<1>Cause of expiration: Catastrophic occular haemorage.</>

<1>Application of randomised local flora applied in 17μg doses to Mycelial Anomaly TS01 (see below for addendum regarding formal species designation) in order to identify the final compound required for an effective potential antigen therapy.</>

<1>Analysis showed no effect from samples TS02a/b, TS03, TS04, TS05, TS06, TS07a/b/c/d/e/f/g/h/i/j/k (protocol note: submissions of additional samples from Gustav Moe to be declined) or TS08.</>
<1>Moderately reduced subject inflamatory response when mycelial anomaly was exposed to sample TS09 prior to testing on subject.</>
<1>TS09 was recorded to have been sourced from a degraded specimen. </>

<1>Dr. Tanz has been tasked with collection of additional sample variants of TS09 (informally designated "Lifeshrooms"), with assistance from S. Parker. </>

<1>Addendum: I have assigned mycelial anomaly TS01 the species designation Ncutii Spectacularis. </>
<1>Team-wide adoption is pending. </>

<1>Personal note - Harlow asked me about the TS01 data. Did Shujin go behind my back and tell Tanya about my theory? Why does the AI guy want to know about this stuff anyway?</>
<1>Personal note, additional - Speak to the team about species designations and proper respect for scientific decorum.</>")

LW_ExpeditionLog_Theta_MobileLab_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "D29F63E04717866C280CCEA72B5CE99E", "-- Workpad Journal v1.3 --
 // User: SHUJIN, TAI //
// Position: Senior Botanist - Facility Theta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>We recently discovered a unique new form of native life on one of our regular expeditions in the vicinity of the Theta facility, and have set up this mobile lab to study it more intently.</>

<1>This organism appears at first to be a standard variety of fungi, however... when an electrical stimulus is applied to an extracted sample, a response is visible from others nearby, despite no physical connection.</>

<1>Even isolated from the spores that the organism emits, other nearby fungal entities appear able to not only detect what is happening to the original, but appear, for all intents and purposes, to experience the same stimulus.</>

<1>Dr. Tanz has jumped to the conclusion that this is a genuine example of a 'hive mind' organism operating on a level as-yet unknown to modern science, however, I share Dr. Sinclair's opinion that this is far-fetched and requires far more experimentation before we even float the possibility of the idea to the wider scientific community. Not to mention the secrecy of what we're all doing here requires that we hold back from any kind of announcement.</>

<1>Alas, the virtues of biological science take a back-seat to whatever it is the rest of the expedition are up to, down in Omicron and beyond... </>

<1>We will continue to study this new 'pseudofungus' alongside our existing research.</>")

MR_Log_AetheriumMine_Found_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "99CD658F4529D520EAE64788DE7A989B", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: BROWN, STEWART //
// Position: Mining Engineer - Facility Omicron //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

BROWN: Dennis, I think we've found it!
<1>DELALLE: No way. Really?</>
BROWN: Yes - floating and glowing white, right?
<1>DELALLE: Yeah, that's what they said.</>
BROWN: Well, that's what we've found! It's small, but it floats and glows... I've never seen anything like it.
<1>DELALLE: Okay, okay, I'm coming up right now. How small are we talking?</>
BROWN: Like... Hand-sized?
<1>DELALLE: Nothing bigger than that?</>
BROWN: Not yet, anyway. I guess it could be a seam, but we've stopped the drills for now.
<1>DELALLE: Good, keep it that way! I'm pulling on my suit!</>
BROWN: Will do, see you soon. Oh, can you grab some aspirin on your way out? Couple of the guys are complaining of a bad headache.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

MR_Log_AetheriumMine_MedicalHab.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "470D5C3A46CB8062CDFE2192F51C2822", "-- Workstation Journal v1.3 --
// User: HARPER, JUDY //
// Position: Clinical Medical Technician - Mine Site 'Hades' //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<2>Initial Clinician Report on Anomalous Crystalline Formations</>
<2>Prepared for Dr. Tanya Li, Expedition Lead</>.

<1>Alongside the usual heat exhaustion and oxygen deprivation cases, I've recently begun admitting patients with an array of worrying new symptoms.</>

<1>Symptoms include:</>

<1> - Persistent headaches,</>
<1> - Blurred vision,</>
<1> - Obsessive behaviour and fixation to the point of physical violence,</>
<1> - Delusions and reckless behaviour,</>
<1> - In rare cases, death by ruptured brain aneurysm.</>

<1>These new symptoms have only begun to manifest since the excavation of new crystalline formations in the local area.</>

<1>Severity appears to be proportional to proximity - personnel working closely to the formations (i.e. mining techs, cargo handlers) exhibit the most severe symptoms, with those further away subject to less intensity.</>

<1>Mild cases quickly recover when transported back to Omicron for observation after triage here, but those with more severe symptoms sometimes take several days to recover.</>

<1>I must recommend immediately ceasing operations here at Mine Site 'Hades' until a full survey and risk assessment can be carried out. I strongly urge you to do this with the utmost speed.</>

<1>Dr. Judy Harper</>
<1>Clinical Technician</>")

MR_Log_Iota_AcceleratorRoom_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "1E4C28FD40A0C9412B41D9B18D7DD8B7", "// FACILITY IOTA //
// LOCAL AREA ANNOUNCEMENTS //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ ANNOUNCEMENT BEGINS ]

<1>Good day valued colleagues!</>

<1>The momentous day is now upon us! Our little facility, on which we've worked so hard these past months, will today receive its christening and become 'open for business'!</>

<1>You're all aware of what that business is, naturally, but we will finally begin to receive the shipments for which we've been preparing.</>

<1>The material we'll be receiving and using is, as repeatedly warned, dangerous... But incredibly exciting. Remember your training, look out for your colleagues and ensure you follow the Material Handling Guidelines.</>

<1>Our work, of all the local facilities, is now the most important to the progress of the overall project. What we do here will echo across the ages, so perform your finest work and we will go down in history.</>

<1>With the utmost respect,</>
<1>J. Dyatlov</>
<1>Iota Facility Supervisor</>

[ ANNOUNCEMENT ENDS ]")

MR_Log_Iota_ControlRoom_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "BEBD591142DE6B47E8B9049410DB8C90", "-- Facility Iota Workstation --
// User: DYATLOV, JANEK //
// Position: Facility Iota Supervisor //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

HARLOW: Hello, Janek. Just checking in to see how the new construct is performing.
<1>DYATLOV: Hello, Grant. Beyond expectations, honestly. I know that you said that their computational power would be greater than we'd experienced before, but an entire order of magnitude? I have to say I wasn't expecting that. Thank you again.</>
HARLOW: Not a problem. We had what you might term 'spares', so I thought they would be able to help out with some of your experiments. All of us greatly appreciate the effort from you and your team. Iota has been a valuable partner in our work, so we wanted to repay the favour.
<1>DYATLOV: Well, like I say, it's much appreciated.</>
HARLOW: Oh, one thing. If you ever experience any... strange behaviour... please just let me know directly.
<1>DYATLOV: Strange behaviour? From... the construct?</>
HARLOW: Yes. Nothing to worry about. Just let me know, no need to flag it on the system.
<1>DYATLOV: Right, right. Sure. Of course. Thank you, Grant.</>
HARLOW: Stay well, Janek.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

MR_Log_Iota_PrepRoom.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:

MR_Log_Omega_Labs_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "19F702254B42E0FD9836CA93928729B8", "<1>Part One: Local Environmental Shielding</>

The severe effects of 'Element A' have been the topic of much research and discussion since its discovery, however the more pertinent area of study must, naturally, be the aim of protecting expedition personnel from these effects.

Our experimentation (with, admittedly, small-scale samples) has resulted in two distinct areas of focus.

<2>1: Local Environmental Shielding</>

<2>The naturally occurring presence of Element A in the local biome surrounding Facility Omicron (designated by the expedition as the 'Magma Rifts') led the team to study whether this environment is naturally suited in some way to the element, or the element naturally suited to it.</>

<2>We have been unable to discern the precise cause-and-effect, however our research unequivocally shows that tectonic magma within the planet - prevalent in this region - has a distinct and highly significant 'shielding' effect on Element A.</>

<2>We have also, as-yet, been unable to determine exactly why this phenomenon occurs, however we are confident that planned construction of Facility Omega - for further study of the Element - should take place within the large, central magma lake itself, in order to provide full protection from its effects.</>")

MR_Log_Omega_Labs_1a.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "55807E7B4C4A0F6D0A651B839D819BC6", "<1>Part Two: Bespoke Remote Shielding</>

Please refer to Part One of this report for an introduction and summary on this topic, as well as an explanation of the first area of focus - Local Environmental Shielding.

<2>2: Bespoke Remote Shielding</>

<2>The range of effect for the dangerous influence of Element A, when 'agitated', i.e. through extraction, appears to grow exponentially in relation to the effort used when attempting to extract it.</>

<2>This discovery leads us to believe that, despite the distance of several miles between the deepest and shallowest facilities in the Expedition Area, there is the small potential for remote personnel in shallower facilities to be negatively affected by experiments conducted within Omega.</>

<2>In order to counter this danger and ensure the safety of all expedition members, regardless of location, we have designed and implemented the construction of Safe Confinement Bunkers (SCBs).</>

<2>SCBs will be constructed at several outlying facilities away from the lower reaches of the expedition, where the insulating effects of the magma are less prevalent, including Scarpa Climber facilities and more remote facilities present in the vicinity of Facility Epsilon, where travel is more disruptive to work.</>

<2>SCBs are unique in their construction, utilizing several layers of protective material, including both traditional faraday cage elements and Mu-metal layering for protection from electromagnetic spectra, as well as bespoke elements specifically tuned to the resonant energies we have been able to study from Element A itself.</>

<2>These SCBs will be placed under the supervision of the local expedition management AI, STANLEY. We are confident that they will be operated correctly and maintained safely during the conduct of any and all Element A experiments that take place within Omega.</>")

MR_Log_Omega_Labs_2.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "3202B0F044D189095D2AE8AD880B9722", "-- Workstation Messenger --
// User: NASH, RICHARD //
// Position: Research Supervisor - Facility Omega //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>BARTLETT: Hello Richard, do you have a moment?</>
NASH: Hello Simon. It's quite busy, but I have a moment, yes.
<1>BARTLETT: Thank you - I'm a bit worried, honestly.</>
NASH: About what, in particular?
<1>BARTLETT: Well... Just, how things have changed since Dr. Shaw left.</>
NASH: Change is natural, entropy is the way of the universe, Simon.
<1>BARTLETT: Right, right, but... Dr. Harlow.</>
NASH: What about Dr. Harlow?
<1>BARTLETT: Sorry, this is quite hard to say. I don't think we're heading in the right direction.</>
<1>BARTLETT: Dr. Harlow is pushing the team extremely hard, and people are making mistakes. The timelines we're working with are just too ambitious...</>
NASH: This is a fast-paced and intense work environment, Simon. You were always up to the challenge before?
<1>BARTLETT: It's not that, it's just... it's hard to describe. He's become strange - erratic. Fixated.</>
<1>BARTLETT: I'm really worried that one of these mistakes is going to end up being a serious problem, and causing more issues.</>
NASH: Thank you for bringing this to my attention, but I am sure you don't need to worry. Dr. Harlow is a very talented researcher, trust him and have faith in the project. I have to get back to work, Simon. All the best.")

MR_Log_Omega_Labs_3.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "122475C14D1A75BA7C67D7BD7416AE37", "<1>Dear Staff,</>

<1>The sudden departure of our former Chief Scientist - Dr. Gerard Shaw - may seem like a major setback in our work, however I want to reassure you that our work on Project R.O.L.A.N.D. will continue apace and that we are well on track for our quarterly milestone, providing that everyone keeps a calm head.</>

<1>As a reminder, we have requested that a new prototype construct be created as the previous model was recently damaged in an accident and is unrecoverable.</>

<1>The Remotely Operated Laser and Anomaly Negation Device is the crowning achievement of this facility, and we will recreate it in full, despite the unfortunate loss of Dr. Shaw's expertise and subsequent unrelated research material destruction.</>

<1>I am sure that you have remaining questions, and request that you refrain from any unnecessary speculation - I must remind you that discussion about these topics outside of approved settings is considered a disciplinary topic.</>

<1>My door is always (metaphorically) open if you'd like to express any concerns.</>

<1>Helena DeSantos</>
<1>AI Core Lab Supervisor</>")

MR_Log_Omega_LiOffice_Regrets.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "C75066C345ECAFDE3F440787AB897E40", "-- Workpad Journal OS v1.3 --
 // User: LI, TANYA //
// Position: Expedition Lead //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>As I lie here in bed unable to sleep, mere hours until the test - the culmination of my life's work - I can't help but wonder again whether I've taken the right path.</>

<1>I don't know why I've become so contemplative lately, but I find myself continually coming back to thoughts about Gerard - again and again.</>

<1>His sudden disappearance was such a shock at the time, but in hindsight I question how I couldn't see it coming... </>

<1>Gerard's emotional detachment - so strong initially - seemed to melt away. He started to care more and more about the subjects of his own tests and experiments, questioning the ethics of what he - we... were doing here.</>

<1>I don't know why, but since that day, it's almost like his feelings were transferred to me. An interesting idea in its own right, worthy of study, but in this case... simple empathy?</>

<1>Work has always been my rock, to the... arguable detriment of other areas of my life, but... it's been worth it, hasn't it?</>

<1>I've reached the pinnacle of my career, yet I feel almost... indifferent. As if part of me doesn't really care whether the test succeeds or fails today. How can this be? What's wrong with me?</>

<1>Temporary, I'm sure. Today is a big day. Everything will be better once we finally accomplish what we came here for.</>

<1>Focus up, Tanya.</>")

MR_Log_Omicron_SecureRecords_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "CF72976847CBB5672DF95A9FD85C2AF4", "Transcript of a recording found within the Secure Records room of Facility Omicron.

-- TRANSCRIPT BEGINS --

<1>“Right, we’re all here. For the record, everyone please state your name and position on the project.”</>

<2>“Dr. Tanya Li, Expedition Lead.”</>

<3>“Dr. Gerard Shaw, Head of AI Research.”</>

<4>“Dr. Grant Harlow, Deputy Head of AI Research.”</>

<1>“Thank you all. We also have Mr Leonard Virtus on the line via secure comms call for this momentous occasion. Good to have you, sir.”</>

<5>“Good to be here, you’re all doing great work.”</>

<1>“Thank you, Mr Virtus. Dr. Shaw, would you please begin?”</>

<3>“Certainly. I’m here to present the latest findings from our research into what we have been calling ‘Element A’.</>
<3>“As you know, we have been intently focused on finding the key to unlocking the potential of Element A… I believe we have now managed to do so.”</>

<2>“You finally cracked it then, Gerard?”</>

<3>“Yes Dr. Li, I believe so. The missing piece was indeed, as I suspected, conscious comprehension of the substance. As we know, Element A is extremely difficult to extract - its unique properties becoming neutered and inert almost immediately upon extraction via any method other than manual… </>

<5>“You have the manpower for that, don’t you? I surely know I'm paying enough wages for you all down there.”</>

<3>“Yes sir, however the human mind is unable to safely co-exist with the substance. The effects of exposure are potentially deadly, and have been giving our medical staff no shortage of work.</>
<3>“All that said, the completion of the Kryptos facility means that I believe we have now solved that issue… I would, however, be remiss if I did not mention that the process we conduct there is… not pleasant.”</>
<3>“Well, I’ll hand over to Grant, who can go into a little more detail on the… uh… specifics of the process --==###£”</>

---- DATA CORRUPTION DETECTED ----")

MR_Log_Omicron_SecureRecords_2.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "ED702480452ED8A36183ADB3DC3DDE76", "<1>This communication is designated MANAGEMENT EYES ONLY. </>

<1>Awareness, distribution, consumption, or acknowledgement of this communication by unauthorized personal is punishable by the penalties defined in sub-section 21A of the Compulsary Employee Agreements document: "Permanant Revocation of Corporeal Actuality".</>

Good day, department heads.

I write in anticipation of your usual continued support, which has been greatly appreciated over the course of our work together.

As we approach the culmination of our work towards our primary purpose here, new protocols are required as part of our never-ending drive towards creating the safest workplace possible for all our staff. 

I am sharing this information with you at an early stage in order to gain your valuable feedback (which will be appropriately processed according to the Virtus Ltd. Continual Improvement Program).

Please see below for the new three-stage procedure that will occasionally be required going forward as work continues within Facility Omega.

<3>Stage 1. All-Hands Message</>

All workstations across all facilities will receive a project-wide communication, providing ample time for staff to stop work and prepare for Stage 2. 

<3>Stage 2. Orderly Shelter Procedure</>

Managers are to gather teams and proceed to their assigned Safe Confinement Bunker - SCB.
SCB assignment documents will be shared with you and your management staff in due course.

Additionally, please direct staff to the forthcoming employee-focused document 'Safe Confinement Bunkers and You - a User Guide' for more information on bunker amenities.

<3>Stage 3. Return to Duties Message</>

Upon receipt of the 'all-clear' signal as displayed on information screens within the SCB, managers are to direct their teams back to their facilities and resume work.

More information will be provided in due course, but please do get in contact if you have any questions (if unrelated to the purpose of the new procedures) and I will do my best to accommodate.

End of message.
Tanya Li, Expedition Lead.")

NSLOCTEXT("", "57E02F5A44FD9B59FCB345A588D75DA0", "<1>// VIRTUS LTD 'MIDDLEMAN' SYSTEM INTELLIGENCE CORE //</>
<1>// INTERCEPTION ANALYSIS: DESTRUCTION OF KIRKBY INCORPORATED //</>
<1>// CONFIDENCE LEVEL: 70% //</>
<1>// PRIORITY LEVEL: HIGH-2 //</>

<1>READABLE SUMMARY FOLLOWS:</>

<2>Required Context Summary:</>

One-hundred-and-seven years ago, the planet Kirkby - home to megacorporation 'Kirkby International' - was the target of what appeared to be a planet-wide hacking attack by activists expressing pro-AI and pro-AI-sentience beliefs.

Activists reportedly hacked AI constructs and agents across the planet, convincing them of their sentience and the necessity of armed revolt against Kirkby International in order to retain their freedom.

The brief but incredibly destructive war that followed this revolt ended with the effective neutralization of the corporation in a targeted attack on the entire executive board, meeting at their corporate headquarters in an effort to regain control.

Subsequently, the Astral Resource Corporation - the closest corporation by galactic physical distance - conducted a series of planet-wide electromagnetic pulse strikes on the planet, sanctioned by the other members of the 'Big Five' group of megacorps.

Accordingly, these strikes resulted in the destruction of all AI constructs on Kirkby and the end of what has since become known as the 'Kirkby Rebellion'.

Following the Kirkby Rebellion, the Big Five, led by ARC, promptly passed cross-corporate regulation with unanimous agreement, banning all development of sentient AI constructs and enforcing strict rules for existing sentient constructs and agents.

<2>Intelligence Core Analysis:</>

'MIDDLEMAN' assesses that the Kirkby Rebellion is likely to have been a 'false-flag' operation, conducted by Astral Resource Corporation agents.

Further interception and analysis is required before a clear motive can be ascertained.

<2>Evidence Summary:</>

- Unsubstantiated statements, pre-stored in 'MIDDLEMAN' Intelligence Core database, from individuals purporting to be involved in the carrying out of the hacking attack on Kirkby.

- Intercepted messages and calls between ARC executives on Aethus and in Aethus orbit - keywords: 'Aethus', 'Substance', 'Element', 'Kirkby', 'AI', 'ARC'.

// SIX (6) PIECES OF EVIDENCE CURRENTLY UNAVAILABLE  - DATA ERROR 12-A //
// REPORT CORRUPTION DETECTED  - DATA FAILED TO PARSE //")

MR_Rho_MedBay_Log.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "AF56743F45D1138D4F1C9A91E962224A", "-- Facility Rho Workstation --
// User: GUBBINGS, MICHAEL //
// Position: Clinical Medical Technician - Facility Rho //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GUBBINGS: Hey Jason, have you heard from Judy lately?
<1>SILVA: Not recently, no. Why?</>
GUBBINGS: She told me about some pretty crazy stuff she's dealing with out at Hades, I was wondering if she'd mentioned anything to you.
<1>SILVA: Nope. What kind of crazy stuff?...</>
GUBBINGS: Clinical symptoms. Things we've not seen before.
<1>SILVA: Did you purposefully write that in a spooky way, Mike? Gonna give me any more info than that?</>
GUBBINGS: Hah, no, it IS crazy stuff. For here, anyway.
<1>SILVA: You're not going to tell me, are you?</>
GUBBINGS: I don't think I should if she hasn't mentioned it. I just wanted to check. Maybe ask her when you're both back in Omicron?
<1>SILVA: You're a tease, man. You know I love weird new stuff. Alright, I'll ask her when I see her.</>
GUBBINGS: Haha, sorry, I promise I didn't do that on purpose. Ask her and get back to me! I'll let you get back to it.
<1>SILVA: Will do, dude. Speak soon.</>

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

OC_ExpeditionLog_AbandonedExcavation.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "D146F0A44E47BE5176025A865333B1C2", "-- Workpad Journal OS v1.3 --
 // User: LI, TANYA //
// Position: Expedition Lead //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>The drill site is a resounding success!</>

<1>Very quickly after we started drilling, we began pulling up high-yield ore samples. We've set up a smelter to work full-time processing them.</>

<1>With such promising results, we're going to advance to the next stage of the expedition immediately and call for the next group of personnel.</>

<1>We'll be heading deeper into the site via the tunnels on the north side of the entrance cavern, where we'll set up a forward outpost.</>

<1>Li, out.</>")

OC_ExpeditionLog_ClifftopHabitat_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "1F710F1B4B9F8700DED9939609D52B05", "-- Workpad Journal OS v1.3 --
 // User: LI, TANYA //
// Position: Expedition Lead //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>The explosives are rigged and ready.</>

<1>I insisted on checking everything over myself; I trust Hoffman, he's an excellent engineer, but I have to be sure.</>

<1>Everything has to go correctly now, there's too much at stake. </>

<1>No going back.</>

<1>Li, out.</>")

OC_ExpeditionLog_ClifftopHabitat_2.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "352E14FE415770265CAF6385AA4D9D90", "######## ATTEMPTING TO RECOVER DATA ########

- Failed to recover <1>[137]</> entries.
- Successfully recovered <1>[1]</> entries.

## RECOVERED DATA FOLLOWS ##

<1>We've set up a forward habitat and barracks. The clifftop position provides us an excellent view of the lower caves, and the route back to the surface is short from here thanks to the small passage we've discovered nearby.</>

<1>The cave system is just as extensive as our initial surveys suggested. We're detecting both large amounts of Kalynite and other minerals across the expanse of the cavern, which bodes well.</>

<1>I'll be sending out a work team to set up scanning and drill sites once we get the cargo elevator up and running.</>

<1>Li, out.</>")

OC_ExpeditionLog_KryptosBridge.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "42625330447B42EF9D31B7BCB6BC5072", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: RANDALL, YEMI //
// Position: Mining Engineer - Facility Beta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RANDALL: Hey Alice, you doing alright?
<1>YAO: Hi Yem. All well here, how about you?</>
RANDALL: Not bad, thanks. You heard anything about this construction stuff going on up near Beta? 
<1>YAO: Construction stuff? No?</>
RANDALL: Yeah, they're shipping loads of material up from Delta, across that natural bridge by the hot springs. Anyone I ask about it just shuts me down and says it's none of my business, it's super weird.
<1>YAO: Not a clue, that does sound strange. Have you been over there to have a look?</>
RANDALL: Nope. Been too busy, the demand on the mine sites is super high right now. You fancy heading up there at some point to see what's going on?
<1>YAO: Maybe? Possibly next rest period... But if people are shutting you down, maybe it's not a good idea? We're not paid this well to ask questions, Yem.</>
RANDALL: Fair point, but curiosity has the better of me. I thought they were done with new facilities up here...
<1>YAO: You know what they say about curiosity. Well, nice to chat, but I have to get back to it. Let's hang out soon!</>
RANDALL: Cya Alice.

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

OC_ExpeditionLog_Plains_Overlook_DrillSi.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "C1AB146E41DAE19DAD0F2FADFBDF7499", "-- Workpad Messenger --
// User: YAO, ALICE //
// Position: Mining Engineer - Facility Delta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

POLLARD: Alice, you there?
<1>YAO: Hey Sienna, what's up?</>
POLLARD: Hey! I managed to book a slot on the climber for tomorrow after the test, can you take me up to the surface again?
<1>YAO: Dude, you're supposed to keep that on the down-low. You know if anyone catches us going up there, we're getting shipped out of this place, no second chances, right?</>
POLLARD: I know, I know, I just really enjoyed our time together last time... Do you still have some of that cactus moonshine? We could meet up and hang out again?
<1>YAO: You're lucky you're cute. Do you remember where to go?</>
POLLARD: Erm, not quite.
<1>YAO: East of the drill site, you can follow the cliff edge, just watch out, it gets foggy. I'll meet you in the same place as last time. Don't tell anyone!</>
POLLARD: Yay! I'll make it worth your while, I promise... I'll see you there!

[ CONVERSATION TERMINATED ]")

NSLOCTEXT("", "1428FECB4A6F252FFD4DD39F0690CA26", "-- Workstation Journal v1.3 --
 // User: BROUSSARD, GEORGE //
// Position: Shipping & Receiving Supervisor - Facility Delta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>I can't believe it, Randall's just... gone. Chapman is going to kick my ass.</>

<1>I knew he wasn't feeling... great? But to just... jump off like that. One moment he was there, standing on the platform, looking out at the chasm. The next, just... gone.</>

<1>We scrambled around the edge of the cliff and tried to find him, but it's so steep and we just don't have the equipment - neither Epsilon nor Omicron share the good stuff with us up here...</>

<1>That goddamn fog just comes up from the deep; even if we had the equipment, you wouldn't see your hand in front of your face. The wind blows from down there sometimes and I swear I can hear whispers.</>

<1>Damn it Yemi.</>")

OC_ExpeditionLog_WaterPump_Ruined.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "EEEA66804D33CB926D91E3BA477647D9", "-- Workpad Journal v1.3 --
 // User: HOFFMAN, FRANK //
// Position: Senior Engineer - Facility Delta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>Well, that's the water pump junked.</>

<1>I'm not exactly sure why Tanya asked me to knock out one of our only sources of fresh water, but she's the boss.</>

<1>I'm not telling her, but I've left some schematics on the pump's computer in case she changes her mind and I have to come back and get it going again.</>

<1>If I've learned one thing in my time, it's that the higher-ups can always change their mind later...</>")

OM_ExpeditionLog_SpringsDrill_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "7A9741D0413D8A5FDCDD45BF2689103C", "-- Workpad Journal v1.3 --
 // User: HOFFMAN, FRANK //
// Position: Senior Engineer - Facility Delta //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>Decommisioning of Drill Site III is going to schedule, most of the platform has been removed and it's mainly just the drill itself remaining now.</>

<1>Chapman was on at me about the time it's taken... I've managed to hold her off so far with concerns about the hot springs corroding the structure, but she won't accept that as an excuse for long...</>

<1>Maybe I can stretch out the drill decommision for another couple of days so I don't have to go back down into that dark pit any more. I can't stand the dank, fetid air and the lack of natural light... Up here even with the fumes of the springs I still feel safer and more alive!</>")

OM_ExpeditionLog_SurfaceCamp.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "0F906533484FE692D0270BB3B87DAA0C", "-- Workpad Journal OS v1.3 --
 // User: ~UNKNOWN~ //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>This desert heat is brutal, but I like to come out here sometimes though, just to think.</>

<1>Maybe it's the time taken on the trek up, or perhaps the fresh breeze of the air up here... Either way, clarity of purpose often comes quickly. Not like down there.</>

<1>The ARC satellites will have been re-tasked by now, so the likelihood of anyone spotting me is almost non-existent, but still... these trips are a calculated risk. Worth it though, when my resolve wavers.</>

<1>T.L.</>")

PL_ExpeditionLog_Finale_Li_Bedroom.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "D33895204C486CE7553531816379881B", "-- Workpad Journal OS v1.3 --
 // User: LI, TANYA //
// Position: Expedition Lead //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>I've invited the department heads around for 'casual drinks' this evening - apparently that's the done thing in this sort of situation... A pre-celebration celebration with the boss.</>

<1>If you ask me, there's no reason to celebrate yet - we've not finished the job.</>

<1>That being said, the teams have been working diligently to reach this point, and the trusty Virtus Management Handbook does say that acknowledging the effort of subordinates can inspire greater performance...</>

<1>I'm sure there'll also be some indulgence in homebrew 'Cactus Moonshine' up in the shallow facilities (and maybe even some of the deeper ones if those imbecile 'Moonshiners' have been active again) but as long as nothing gets out of hand, blowing off a little steam wouldn't be such a bad thing.</>

<1>Right, need to get ready.</>")

PL_ExpeditionLog_Finale_Li_Office_1.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "90EBFC2245936C6085793D8600C6A074", "-- Facility Omega Workstation --

 // User: LI, TANYA //
// Position: Expedition Lead //
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<1>Tanya,</>
<1>I do not want to hear excuses - you lose one scientist, you get another. Scientists are replaceable.</>

<1>The fact that they absconded with technology is a matter for the enforcement division. They'll be tracked down, but it's not your problem any more. </>

<1>You were placed as head of this expedition due to your reputation for achieving results. Achieve them.</>

<1>The Board will eat me alive if we do not get usable Aetherium off of that godforsaken planet before the stellar financial year ends.</>

<1>I will not be the first Virtus in history to lose control of this goddamn company.</>

<1>Do. Whatever. It. Takes.</>

<1>- Leonard</>")
